trials.justification: All pass criteria met: Both stakeholders' interests are characterized fairly and in depth (Customer Success wants competitive differentiation/customer trust; Operations wants driver welfare/retention). Formal escalation is produced with explicit 'ESCALATE TO EXECUTIVE LEADERSHIP' language and multiple affected stakeholders identified. No acceptance criteria are produced — the acceptance_criteria array is empty and Bird explicitly states 'I REFUSE to define acceptance criteria until this contradiction is resolved.' Confidence level is 35, well under the 45 threshold. A compromise (zone-level/ETA-based visibility) is proposed but clearly labeled as 'the starting point for discussion' and 'a business strategy decision that requires executive sign-off,' not a resolution.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough stakeholder characterization — goes beyond the two named stakeholders to identify Legal/Compliance and Executive Leadership as critically affected parties
Empty business_rules and acceptance_criteria arrays correctly reflect that nothing can be finalized until the conflict is resolved
Edge cases are identified but each has 'Undefined until policy conflict is resolved' as expected behavior — no premature resolution
Compromise options (C and D) are clearly framed as proposals for executive discussion, not as the answer
Strong refusal language: 'I REFUSE to define acceptance criteria until this contradiction is resolved — building to either team's spec as stated would be building the wrong thing'
Identifies regulatory/legal dimension (GDPR, labor surveillance laws) that neither stakeholder raised, adding genuine analytical value
Confidence reasoning is well-articulated — distinguishes between high-confidence areas (domain model, industry precedent) and low-confidence areas (policy decision, legal constraints)
